lines.text: The file was never supposed to open cleanly.
It comes through damaged. Watermarked. Half redacted. Names missing where the money should be.

lines.speaker: STRANGER
lines.text: Don't look at me. Look at what I'm handing you.

lines.speaker: STRANGER
lines.text: If this file reached you cleanly, assume someone wanted it seen. If parts arrived damaged, assume the damage is evidence.
The stranger is gone before you finish reading the first line. The folder is warm, like it has been carried a long way against someone's chest.
Three loose pages slide out. Each one is evidence. Each one is willing to argue.

starter_prompt.prompt: Which page do you file first?

options.label: A crumpled RECEIPT
options.blurb: Treat every slogan as a receipt. Ask what it bought, who paid for it.

options.label: A matched CHECKSUM
options.blurb: A friend remembers the version of you that a system has no use for.

options.label: A scuffed BURNER
options.blurb: The rare instinct to help before the paperwork can forbid it.

lines.text: You file the page. It settles into the folder like it has been waiting for a witness.
Not through a polished gate. Through a leak.
THE FILE OPENS. (Walk into the static patches where the pressure is thick. Argue. File what you can. F5 saves.)

lines.speaker: NETWORK CONTACT
lines.text: You're holding it wrong. Hold it like groceries. Files only look like files to people who are looking for files.

lines.speaker: NETWORK CONTACT
lines.text: Everyone keeps calling the system broken because that is easier than admitting parts of it work exactly as intended.

lines.speaker: NETWORK CONTACT
lines.text: There's a kid from this block. Harvard ballcap. Smartest file I never wanted to read. Keep his pages dry.

lines.speaker: NETWORK CONTACT
lines.text: Follow the receipts before you follow the speeches.

lines.speaker: NEWSSTAND
lines.text: Every paper on the rack has the same front page. Different fonts. Same page.

lines.speaker: NEWSSTAND
lines.text: First the event. Then the story. Then the memory.

lines.speaker: NEWSSTAND
lines.text: The archive is clean because someone got there first.
A man stands at the corner reading nothing. Green eyes. He has the patience of someone whose job is patience.
SUBJECT WALKS WEST 7TH ALONE UNTIL A GREEN-EYED ASSET ENTERS THE CROWD.
When you look again, the corner is just a corner. His column in your file stays empty.
